summary: FIDEM iteration 3 verified end-to-end. Backend refactor (server.py 1450 -> 180 lines, split into 6 routers + core.py) is regression-clean: all 43 previous pytest tests still pass. Added 6 new pytest tests for iteration 3 (test_zz_iteration3.py) covering WebSocket connect/ping/invalid-token, real-time message + gift fan-out (sender + recipient both receive WS event), auth rate limiter (11th wrong-pw login = 429), and admin broadcast dry_run. Total backend: 49/49 passing. Frontend WS verified live in browser: after admin login the client opens wss://.../api/ws?token=<jwt> and receives the {type:'connected', user_id} frame. Chat.jsx code-review confirms 5s polling removed (no setInterval; wsEvent useEffect appends incoming messages). Me.jsx bell badge increments on wsEvent.type==='notification'. Rate limit also visually confirmed via frontend toast 'Too many attempts. Try again later.' after burst logins.

minor.issue: Shared in-memory RateLimiter is per-process, not per-pod. Multi-instance deployments will allow up to N*10 attempts. Acceptable for single-pod MVP; flag for prod scale-out. Also: same IP bucket covers BOTH login and register — burst-testing login can block legitimate signups for 5 min.
Content-Length pre-check still not present. Current 8MB check happens after file.read() (auth_r.py). Iteration-2 minor still open. Normal uploads work (verified).
Invalid-token disconnect uses code 4401 but client receives close before any send_json — works but websockets client lib reports code as 1006/1000 depending on ingress. Functionally correct.

action_items: (Carry-over) Add Content-Length pre-check on /files/upload before reading body — still pending from iter 2.
(Minor) Consider scoping rate-limit bucket per-endpoint (login vs register) so burst-test of one doesn't lock the other.

(Test hygiene) test_zz_iteration3.py is named 'zz_' so it runs LAST in pytest alphabetical order — the rate-limit test consumes the IP bucket for 5 minutes; keep this name so combined suite stays green.
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor is clean: server.py 180 lines, core.py has DB/helpers/ConnectionManager/RateLimiter, routers split by domain. All endpoint paths preserved.
ConnectionManager uses asyncio.Lock + dict[uid -> set[WebSocket]] — supports multi-tab correctly (verified by test_message_pushed_to_both: both A and B WS receive the event).
push_notif fires manager.send_to_user via asyncio.create_task (fire-and-forget) — best-effort, won't block API response. Good.
RateLimiter sliding-window over time.monotonic() — correct. In-memory only; not shared across pods (call out for prod).
Broadcast dry_run short-circuits BEFORE the loop (admin_r.py:109) — verified no Telegram DMs sent in dry-run path.
WebSocket auth via ?token=<jwt> query param leaks token to ingress access logs; same trade-off as /api/files/{path}?auth=. Acceptable for MVP, monitor.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_zz_iteration3.py (NEW — 6 tests for WS + rate-limit + dry-run broadcast)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (WS connect verified live; Chat/Me code-reviewed and wired to wsEvent; rate-limit toast verified)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@fidem.uz / Admin@123. Runtime test users registered with random suffix.
seed_data_creation: None added by tests. Backend auto-seeds 1 admin + 14 demo users on startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run order matters: test_zz_iteration3.py MUST run after the other two files (it intentionally burns the auth rate-limit bucket for the test runner's IP for 5 minutes). To run a single iteration_3 test in isolation, just point pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_zz_iteration3.py. WebSocket URL is wss://serious-dating-8.preview.emergentagent.com/api/ws?token=<jwt>. The Kubernetes ingress upgrades WS correctly. Frontend stores JWT at localStorage.fidem_token (no user object stored — decode sub claim from JWT for user_id).
rca of the issue: No issues found in iteration 3. The earlier-flagged Chat 3-dot menu (chat-menu/chat-block/chat-report) was outside the scope of this review (iteration 2 carry-over) — should be re-tested separately if the main agent has since added the JSX.
